summary: Fowler documents a technical issue with his Belkin KVM switch causing erratic mouse behavior when switching to Linux machines. He discovers that changing the mouse protocol configuration in XF86Config from 'IMPS/2' to 'auto' resolves the worst of the problem. The post provides specific configuration file changes that fixed the issue, borrowed from a Morphix install. While the solution isn't perfect—requiring some mouse movement to reactivate after switching—it's a workable fix. He concludes with frustration about X configuration complexity compared to Windows.
key_points: Belkin KVM switch causes mouse to move randomly and click when switching to Linux
Workaround exists by switching virtual consoles (ctrl-alt-f5 then alt-f7) to reset mouse state
Changing mouse protocol from 'IMPS/2' to 'auto' in XF86Config resolves the main issue
The fix requires wiggling the mouse after switching but eliminates the erratic behavior
X Window System configuration remains a significant pain point for Linux desktop users
money_quotes: The worst problem shows itself when I switch into Linux and mouse goes haywire, moving around at random and clicking buttons.
I won't try to figure out what the important change is, I'm pretty ignorant of X and would like to be more so.
One day I hope someone or something will make configuring X as easy as it is to get windows to work.
At the moment this is one of the most painful parts of using Linux as a desktop machine.
key_insight: Even experienced developers sometimes solve configuration problems through trial and error rather than deep understanding, and that's an acceptable approach when it works.
